**Automotive Calculations** are essential for car, motorcycle, and vehicle owners: fuel economy, maintenance costs, tire pressure, power conversions, amortization timelines.

## How to Calculate

Determine your next oil change interval based on oil type. Free and fast.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a qualified professional. Values are current through 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: How often should I change my oil?
faq.a: Mineral every 5,000 km, semi-synthetic every 7,500 km, synthetic every 10-15,000 km, long-life every 20,000 km.

faq.q: What if I don't drive my car often?
faq.a: Even if unused, change your oil every 1-2 years.

faq.q: Should I change the oil filter too?
faq.a: Always change the filter with every oil change.

faq.q: What oil viscosity should I use?
faq.a: 5W-30 is common for modern cars; check your owner's manual for the exact specification.

faq.q: Does severe driving affect oil change intervals?
faq.a: Yes. In city driving or dusty conditions, reduce the interval by 30%.
